title: development object
text: development object A definition for a logical application unit, such as a component, entity, field, message, menu, global variable, and so on. Development objects define the data accessed by the application, the layout and appearance of components, the user interactions, default behavior, and processing performed by the application. During the build process, development objects are compiled into executable runtime objects and libraries, which can be packaged into archives for deployment. Related Links Uniface Development Objects Development objects are application definitions for logical application units, such as components, fields, properties, messages, variables, and so on. During compilation, development objects are used to generate executable runtime objects and libraries, which can be packaged into archives for immediate deployment. runtime object A compiled or generated unit of the application that can be executed, called, or used by the Uniface runtime engine. Runtime objects are generated from development objects and usually deployed in deployed in a Uniface archive. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
